ogTitle: Lajamanu vs Lake Mackay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lajamanu and Lake Mackay.
heading: Lajamanu vs Lake Mackay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lajamanu, Northern Territory and Lake Mackay, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lake Mackay has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Lajamanu (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lajamanu or Lake Mackay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lajamanu has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Lake Mackay scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lajamanu scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Lake Mackay scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lajamanu or Lake Mackay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lake Mackay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
